Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient presents with acute appendicitis. Surgeon performs laparoscopic removal of a non-ruptured appendix. No complications noted.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Laparoscopy, surgical, appendectomy
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Laparoscopic approach → 44970. If open and non-ruptured → 44950. Key: confirm approach (lap vs open).

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Open appendectomy performed for ruptured appendix with localized abscess and peritonitis confirmed intraoperatively.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Appendectomy
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: for ruptured appendix with abscess or generalized peritonitis

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Appendectomy performed incidentally at the time of a primary laparotomy for bowel resection when appendix found to be inflamed.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Appendectomy
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: when done for indicated purpose at time of other major procedure

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: 65-year-old male presents with a reducible right inguinal hernia. Open repair performed with mesh.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Repair initial inguinal hernia, age 5 years or older; reducible
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Age ≥5, initial (not recurrent), reducible = 49505. If incarcerated → 49507. If recurrent → 49520/49521.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient with recurrent right inguinal hernia, incarcerated but not strangulated. Laparoscopic repair performed.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Laparoscopy, surgical; repair recurrent inguinal hernia
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Recurrent + laparoscopic = 49651 regardless of reducible/incarcerated status. Open recurrent = 49520/49521.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient undergoes repair of a ventral incisional hernia, 6 cm defect, initial presentation, reducible. Open approach with mesh.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Repair anterior abdominal hernia(s), initial, 3–10 cm, reducible
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Defect size 3–10 cm, initial, reducible = 49593. Any approach (open/lap/robotic) uses same code family. Mesh use does not change code.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Large incisional hernia, defect > 10 cm, incarcerated, recurrent. Robotic-assisted repair with mesh.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Repair anterior abdominal hernia(s), recurrent, > 10 cm, incarcerated
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Recurrent + >10 cm + incarcerated = 49618. Approach (open/lap/robotic) does NOT change the code.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Elective laparoscopic cholecystectomy for symptomatic cholelithiasis. No common bile duct exploration.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Laparoscopy, surgical; cholecystectomy
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Standard laparoscopic chole without cholangiogram or CBD exploration = 47562.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy with intraoperative cholangiography performed to rule out CBD stones.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Laparoscopy, surgical; cholecystectomy with cholangiography
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Cholangiography included in 47563 — do NOT separately bill imaging guidance.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Open cholecystectomy performed due to conversion from laparoscopic approach after bleeding. Common bile duct explored with stone removal.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Cholecystectomy with exploration of common duct
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Open + CBD exploration = 47610. If CBD exploration also includes choledochoenterostomy → 47612.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient undergoes CABG with 3 venous grafts (saphenous vein), no arterial grafts used.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Coronary artery bypass, vein only; 3 coronary venous grafts

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: CABG performed using 2 arterial grafts (LIMA and radial artery). No venous grafts.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Coronary artery bypass, using arterial graft(s); 2 coronary arterial grafts
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Arterial-only bypass uses 33533–33536. CBGB category = vein only

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: CABG with 1 arterial graft (LIMA) and 2 venous grafts (saphenous). Total 3 grafts.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Arterial graft: 33533 (single). Vein grafts: 33511 (2 venous). Report both.
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Combined arterial + vein bypass: report arterial codes (CBGC) AND vein codes (CBGB) separately based on graft type count.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient undergoes open aortic valve replacement with a tissue prosthesis via median sternotomy with cardiopulmonary bypass.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Replacement, aortic valve, open, with CPB; prosthetic valve
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Standard AVR with tissue prosthesis = 33405. Homograft = 33406. Stentless = 33410. Ross procedure = 33413.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient with severe aortic stenosis who is high surgical risk undergoes TAVR via transfemoral approach.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR), transfemoral approach
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: TAVR approach determines code: transfemoral = 33361, transaortic = 33365, transapical = 33366. Transfemoral is most common.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient undergoes mitral valve repair with annuloplasty ring insertion under cardiopulmonary bypass.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Valvuloplasty, mitral valve, with CPB; with prosthetic ring
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Repair + ring = 33426. Without ring = 33425. Radical reconstruction = 33427. Replacement = 33430.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Elderly patient with displaced femoral neck fracture. Hemiarthroplasty performed with bipolar femoral prosthesis.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Hemiarthroplasty, hip, partial (e.g., femoral stem prosthesis, bipolar arthroplasty)
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Partial hip replacement (femoral component only) = 27125. Full THR = 27130. Fracture context doesn't change this — it's still 27125.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient with end-stage osteoarthritis undergoes primary total hip arthroplasty with uncemented acetabular and femoral components.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Arthroplasty, acetabular and proximal femoral prosthetic replacement (total hip arthroplasty)
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Both components replaced = 27130. Cementing method does not change the code.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient with failed prior THR presents for revision. Both femoral and acetabular components are revised.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Revision of total hip arthroplasty
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: both components

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient undergoes total knee arthroplasty (TKA) for bilateral end-stage knee OA. Bilateral procedure performed under same anesthetic.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Arthroplasty, knee, medial AND lateral compartments with or without patella (TKA) — bilateral modifier
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Bilateral same-day TKA = 27447 with modifier -50 (bilateral) or list twice with -RT/-LT. Unilateral = 27447 no modifier.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Revision of total knee arthroplasty
CPT Description (Abbreviated): both the femoral and entire tibial components replaced due to aseptic loosening.
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Revision TKA

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient undergoes laparoscopic total hysterectomy for fibroids. Uterus weight 200 g. Tubes and ovaries preserved.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Laparoscopy, surgical, with total hysterectomy, for uterus 250 g or less
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: LAVH total, uterus ≤250 g, no adnexa removed = 58570. With adnexa = 58571. >250 g = 58572/58573.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Open total abdominal hysterectomy (TAH) for endometrial cancer. Para-aortic and pelvic lymph node sampling performed.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Total abdominal hysterectomy, including partial vaginectomy, with para-aortic and pelvic lymph node sampling
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Oncologic TAH with lymph node sampling = 58200. Standard TAH without lymph nodes = 58150.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient undergoes laparoscopic supracervical hysterectomy. Uterus weighed 310 g. Both ovaries removed.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Laparoscopy, surgical, supracervical hysterectomy, uterus > 250 g; with removal of tube(s) and/or ovary(s)
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Supracervical + laparoscopic + >250 g + adnexa removed = 58544. Without adnexa = 58543. ≤250 g with adnexa = 58542.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient with lumbar spinal stenosis undergoes laminectomy at L4-L5 (1 segment) without discectomy or facetectomy.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Laminectomy with decompression of spinal cord/cauda equina, 1-2 segments; lumbar (except spondylolisthesis)
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Lumbar, 1-2 segments, decompression only = 63005. Add facetectomy/foraminotomy = 63047. More than 2 segments = 63017.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Cervical discectomy with decompression at C5-C6, single level, anterior approach.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Discectomy, anterior, with decompression of spinal cord; cervical, single interspace
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Anterior cervical discectomy = 63075. Each additional level = 63076 (add-on). Posterior = different code family.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient with acute subdural hematoma. Emergency craniotomy performed for evacuation. Supratentorial location.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Craniectomy or craniotomy for evacuation of hematoma, supratentorial; extradural or subdural
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Subdural hematoma evacuation, supratentorial = 61312. Intracerebral = 61313. Infratentorial = 61314/61315.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient with supratentorial glioblastoma. Craniotomy with bone flap for tumor excision, not a meningioma.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Craniectomy/craniotomy; for excision of brain tumor, supratentorial, except meningioma
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Supratentorial tumor excision (non-meningioma) = 61510. Meningioma = 61512. Infratentorial = 61518 series.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient with peripheral vascular disease and gangrene. Below-knee amputation (BKA) through tibia and fibula performed.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Amputation, leg, through tibia and fibula
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Standard BKA = 27880. With immediate cast = 27881. Open/guillotine = 27882. AKA = 27590 series.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Transmetatarsal amputation of foot for diabetic foot osteomyelitis.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Amputation, foot; transmetatarsal
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Transmetatarsal foot amputation = 28805. Midtarsal (Chopart) = 28800. Single toe = 28820/28825.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Cesarean delivery only, primary, no antepartum or postpartum care included.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Cesarean delivery only
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Delivery only (no ante/postpartum) = 59514. With postpartum = 59515. Full OB package = 59510.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Repeat cesarean delivery following failed trial of labor after previous C-section (TOLAC). Full OB package.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Routine OB care including antepartum, cesarean delivery, and postpartum — following attempted VBAC
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: TOLAC → repeat C/S with full OB care = 59618. Delivery only = 59620. With postpartum only = 59622.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Patient with infrarenal AAA undergoes open repair with tube prosthesis after failed EVAR.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Open repair of infrarenal AAA following unsuccessful endovascular repair
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: tube prosthesis

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Direct open repair of infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm, not ruptured, no visceral or iliac involvement.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Direct repair of aneurysm; for aneurysm and associated occlusive disease, abdominal aorta
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Non-ruptured, non-visceral, non-iliac AAA = 35081. Ruptured = 35082. Involving iliac = 35102/35103.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Robot-assisted laparoscopic radical prostatectomy (RALP) with bilateral nerve sparing for localized prostate cancer.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Laparoscopy, surgical prostatectomy, retropubic radical, including nerve sparing, includes robotic assistance
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: RALP = 55866 (robotic assistance explicitly included in descriptor). Open retropubic radical = 55840.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Robot-assisted radical prostatectomy with bilateral pelvic lymphadenectomy (external iliac, hypogastric, obturator).
CPT Description (Abbreviated): RALP with bilateral pelvic lymphadenectomy
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: RALP + full bilateral pelvic lymph node dissection = 55869. Limited LN biopsy = 55868. Without lymph nodes = 55866.

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: New permanent pacemaker insertion with transvenous atrial AND ventricular leads (dual chamber system).
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Insertion of new permanent pacemaker with transvenous electrode(s)
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: atrial and ventricular

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Existing dual-lead pacemaker battery depleted. Pulse generator replacement only
CPT Description (Abbreviated): leads tested and left in place.
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Removal of permanent pacemaker pulse generator with replacement

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy for diverticular disease with primary anastomosis.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Laparoscopy, surgical; colectomy, partial, with anastomosis
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Laparoscopic partial colectomy with anastomosis = 44204 regardless of segment (sigmoid, right, etc.).

Clinical Scenario / Operative Note Summa: Open Hartmann's procedure — sigmoid resection with end colostomy and closure of rectal stump for perforated diverticulitis.
CPT Description (Abbreviated): Colectomy, partial; with end colostomy and closure of distal segment (Hartmann type procedure)
Key Differentiators / Decision Rules: Hartmann = 44143. Open with anastomosis = 44140. Laparoscopic Hartmann = 44206.
